Open and inspect the full repo: chinopardo/CA_Trading_System_EA to double-check and audit the full repo: chinopardo/CA_Trading_System_EA, if the new specs(open and read the attached "Backend Integration Plan.json" for the new specs) have been applied correctly:


Goal:
Create an implementation plan that computes the full raw signal inventory, groups signals into Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume, and Volatility categories, selects exactly one active signal per category, converts selected category signals into a true signal-stack with category pass/fail states, applies a hard location gate from Market/Institutional Structures, and adds formal fallback, backup, and degrade logic when one Institutional signal is missing or when the whole Institutional category is unavailable, especially for FOREX, XAUUSD, GOLD, and other fragmented OTC markets.


Context:
For FOREX and XAUUSD, assume OTC venue fragmentation. Spot FX and most FX derivatives are OTC, decentralized, fragmented across venues, and much customer flow is internalised rather than fully visible to the broader market. BIS describes FX as OTC, decentralized, fragmented, and increasingly internalised, while LBMA describes the London gold market as a wholesale OTC market and publishes trade data to improve transparency rather than offering a single consolidated public order book. U.S. ATS and off-exchange transparency is delayed, not real-time. Use Cont-Kukanov-Stoikov style OFI and depth-impact structure. Use standard TA-Lib-style RSI, MACD, ADX, ATR, StochRSI, EMA, and Bollinger definitions. The architecture must avoid indicator echo by selecting one active signal from each category, then combining those selected category signals with always-on Market/Institutional Structures for FinalIntegratedStateVector_t. The selected category signals must feed a mandatory SignalStackGate_t and a mandatory LocationPass_t before alpha, execution, and risk routing. The fallback/degrade logic must never fabricate missing DOM, Order Flow, OBI, OFI, or trade-sign truth from candle-only data; it must first use valid direct signals, then valid proxy signals, then explicit stack-relax or hard-block rules. (Bank for International Settlements)


Constraints:
Preserve every formula below exactly and add the degrade logic below without omitting any prior signal family.
Add explicit grouping for Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume, and Volatility.
Add explicit EMA, ATR, and Bollinger formulas and category membership.
Compute all raw signals, even if only one signal per category is selected.
Do not require every signal in a category to be true at the same time.
Use one of two selection modes:
SelectionMode = "fixed" or SelectionMode = "dynamic"
In fixed mode, the active category signal is chosen from config.
In dynamic mode, score_{j,t} = valid_{j,t} * abs(z(x_{j,t})) * w_j, sel(c,t) = argmax_{j in G_c} score_{j,t}, CatSignal_{c,t} = x_{sel(c,t),t}
If no candidate in category c is valid, CatSignal_{c,t} = 0 and CategoryActive_{c,t} = 0
Keep DOM, Order Flow, OBI, and OFI as distinct raw signals even if only one Institutional candidate is active.
Keep raw VPIN_t, Resil_t, SpreadShock_t, Slippage_t, DepthFade_t, and InternalisationProxy_t available for hard execution, and risk vetoes even if they are not the selected Institutional signal.
Base market variables are always computed and always available.
Market/Institutional Structures are always computed and always appended to the active state vector.
Correlation and dark-pool/internalisation proxies must be computed and stored; they may be appended to an auxiliary context vector or used by config.
Use z(x_t) = (x_t - mean(x)) / (stdev(x) + eps) for normalization.
Use venue-aware aggregation for FX/XAUUSD and direct centralized-book logic for futures, equities, and crypto when available.
Do not omit, merge, or rename any formula-defined feature.
Do not use TrendSlope_t as both a default Trend candidate and a default Structure feature; keep it computed in RawSignalBank_t and use it in StructureVector_t by default.
By default, use anti-echo subfamily selection inside the Institutional category so one Institutional signal is still selected overall, but the category does not become its own echo chamber.
Add mandatory fallback/degrade logic for:


One missing Institutional signal inside an otherwise available Institutional category.

One missing Institutional subfamily inside an otherwise available Institutional category.

The whole Institutional category unavailable.
For FX/XAUUSD/GOLD and other OTC-first markets, default InstitutionalDegradeMode = "proxy_substitute_then_stack_relax".
For centralized futures, equities, and crypto with expected direct market data, default InstitutionalDegradeMode = "hard_block" or "proxy_substitute" by config.
The decision stack must be:
RawSignalBank_t -> CategorySelectedVector_t -> CategoryPassVector_t -> SignalStackGate_t -> LocationPass_t -> FinalIntegratedStateVector_t -> alpha_t, exec_t, risk_t -> TradeGate_t


Formula assumptions:
Use OFI and depth-impact structure consistent with Cont-Kukanov-Stoikov order-book event modeling, with OFI linearly linked to short-horizon price changes and the slope inversely related to depth.
Use standard TA-Lib-style RSI, MACD, ADX, ATR, EMA, StochRSI, and Bollinger definitions.
Use helper identities where needed:
Depth_t = DOMTot_t^(K) or the venue-aggregated equivalent
DeltaCVD_t = CVD_t - CVD_{t-1}
VWAP_t = VWAP_[sessionStart,t]
TWAP_t = TWAP_[sessionStart,t]
For Direction mapping:
Dir_t in {-1, +1}
For signed continuous signals, DirMap(x_t) = sign(x_t)
For RSI, DirMap(RSI_t) = 1 if RSI_t > 50 + bandRSI, -1 if RSI_t < 50 - bandRSI, else 0
For StochRSI, DirMap(StochRSI_t) = 1 if StochRSI_t > 50 + bandStoch, -1 if StochRSI_t < 50 - bandStoch, else 0
For ADX, DirMap(ADX_t) = sign(PlusDI_t - MinusDI_t) if ADX_t >= thADX, else 0
For VAState_t and TPOState_t, DirMap(x_t) = x_t
For volatility candidates, use RegimeMap(x_t) rather than directional sign:
RegimeMap(ATR_t) = 1 if ATR_t in [thATRMin, thATRMax], else 0
RegimeMap(BBWidth_t) = 1 if BBWidth_t in [thBBWidthMin, thBBWidthMax], else 0
RegimeMap(RV_t) = 1 if RV_t in [thRVMin, thRVMax], else 0
RegimeMap(BV_t) = 1 if BV_t in [thBVMin, thBVMax], else 0
RegimeMap(Jump_t) = 1 if Jump_t <= thJumpMax, else 0
RegimeMap(sigmaP_t) = 1 if sigmaP_t in [thSigmaPMin, thSigmaPMax], else 0
RegimeMap(sigmaGK_t) = 1 if sigmaGK_t in [thSigmaGKMin, thSigmaGKMax], else 0
RegimeMap(BBPos_t) = 1 if BBWidth_t in [thBBWidthMin, thBBWidthMax], else 0
Because FX and wholesale gold are fragmented OTC markets and U.S. ATS transparency is delayed, degrade logic must prefer quote-based, auction/profile, and structure-based proxies only when direct Institutional observability is missing; it must not pretend that missing book or signed-flow data exists. (Bank for International Settlements)


Required output:
Build seven layers:


RawSignalBank_t

CategorySelectedVector_t

CategoryPassVector_t

SignalStackGate_t

LocationPass_t

FinalIntegratedStateVector_t

alpha_t, exec_t, risk_t, TradeGate_t

Base market variables
bid_t = best bid
ask_t = best ask
spread_t = ask_t - bid_t
mid_t = (ask_t + bid_t) / 2
relSpread_t = (ask_t - bid_t) / (mid_t + eps)
bidSize_t = best bid size
askSize_t = best ask size
micro_t = (ask_t * bidSize_t + bid_t * askSize_t) / (bidSize_t + askSize_t + eps)


If venue aggregated:
midAgg_t = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * mid_{t,v}
spreadAgg_t = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * spread_{t,v}
sum_{v=1..V} w_v = 1


Institutional raw signal bank
Institutional raw signals are computed separately. Only one becomes InstSel_t in CategorySelectedVector_t. All raw Institutional signals remain available in RawSignalBank_t.


2.1 Order Books and explicit Depth of Market
DOMBid_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t}
DOMAsk_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t}
DOMTot_t^(K) = DOMBid_t^(K) + DOMAsk_t^(K)
DOMSkew_t^(K) = (DOMBid_t^(K) - DOMAsk_t^(K)) / (DOMBid_t^(K) + DOMAsk_t^(K) + eps)
DOMPressure_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * (B_{k,t} - A_{k,t})
Use w_k = exp(-alphaDepth * (k - 1)) or another monotone decay.


If OTC venue aggregated:
DOMBidAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * DOMBid_{t,v}^(K)
DOMAskAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * DOMAsk_{t,v}^(K)
DOMSkewAgg_t^(K) = (DOMBidAgg_t^(K) - DOMAskAgg_t^(K)) / (DOMBidAgg_t^(K) + DOMAskAgg_t^(K) + eps)


2.2 Explicit Order Flow
eps_i in {-1, +1}
flow_i = eps_i * vol_i
BuyFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} I(eps_i = +1) * vol_i
SellFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} I(eps_i = -1) * vol_i
NetFlow_[t-h,t] = BuyFlow_[t-h,t] - SellFlow_[t-h,t]
FlowImb_[t-h,t] = (BuyFlow_[t-h,t] - SellFlow_[t-h,t]) / (BuyFlow_[t-h,t] + SellFlow_[t-h,t] + eps)
SignedFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} eps_i * vol_i
CVD_t = CVD_{t-1} + sum_{i in (t-dt,t]} eps_i * vol_i
DeltaCVD_t = CVD_t - CVD_{t-1}


2.3 Order Book Imbalance, explicit OBI
OBI_t^(1) = (bidSize_t - askSize_t) / (bidSize_t + askSize_t + eps)
OBI_t^(K) = (sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t} - sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t}) / (sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t} + sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t} + eps)


If OTC venue aggregated:
OBIAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * OBI_{t,v}^(K)


Keep the alias explicit:
OBI_t^(1) = DOMSkew_t^(1)


2.4 Event-based OFI
e_n = I(P_n^B >= P_{n-1}^B) * q_n^B - I(P_n^B <= P_{n-1}^B) * q_{n-1}^B - I(P_n^A <= P_{n-1}^A) * q_n^A + I(P_n^A >= P_{n-1}^A) * q_{n-1}^A
OFI_t = sum_{n in W_t} e_n
DeltaMid_t = beta_t * OFI_t + err_t


2.5 Rolling impact coefficients
beta_t = argmin_beta sum_{u=t-L+1..t} omega_u * (DeltaMid_u - beta * OFI_u)^2
Qsign_u = sum_{i in u} eps_i * vol_i
lambda_t = argmin_lambda sum_{u=t-L+1..t} omega_u * (DeltaMid_u - lambda * Qsign_u)^2


Alternative concave-size impact form:
DeltaMid_u = lambda_t * sign(Q_u) * abs(Q_u)^psi + eta_u
0 < psi <= 1


2.6 Toxicity filter
For bucket size Vstar:
Vplus_b = buy volume in bucket b
Vminus_b = sell volume in bucket b
ImbBucket_b = abs(Vplus_b - Vminus_b) / Vstar
VPIN_t = (1 / N) * sum_{b=t-N+1..t} ImbBucket_b


2.7 Absorption, replenishment, resiliency
AbsPlus_t = VaggrPlus_{W_t} / (1 + abs(DeltaMid_{W_t}) / dp)
AbsMinus_t = VaggrMinus_{W_t} / (1 + abs(DeltaMid_{W_t}) / dp)
ReplBid_t = (DepthBid_post - DepthBid_min) / (Vsell_shock + eps)
ReplAsk_t = (DepthAsk_post - DepthAsk_min) / (Vbuy_shock + eps)
Resil_t = exp(-tau_spread / tauSpreadBar) * exp(-tau_depth / tauDepthBar)


2.8 Metaorder impact and execution
Impact_t(Q) = Y * sigma_D * sqrt(Q / (V_D + eps))
Impact_t(Q, partRate) = Y * sigma_D * sqrt(Q / (V_D + eps)) * f(partRate)


Execution schedule:
min_{n_k} E(C) + LambdaRisk * Var(C)
subject to X_k = X_{k-1} - n_k, X_0 = Q, X_N = 0
S_k = S_{k-1} + sigma * sqrt(tau) * xi_k + gamma * n_k
ExecPrice_k = S_k + eta * (n_k / tau)
C = sum_{k=1..N} n_k * ExecPrice_k - Q * S_0


2.9 VWAP, TWAP, POV
VWAP_[0,T] = sum_i p_i * vol_i / (sum_i vol_i + eps)
TWAP_[0,T] = (1 / N) * sum_{k=1..N} p_k
ContinuousTWAP_[0,T] = (1 / T) * integral_0^T p(t) dt
n_t = min(Xrem_t, rho * Vmarket_t)


Expected volume curve:
integral_0^T u(t) dt = 1
x'(t) = Q * u(t)


Cumulative POV target:
POVTargetCum_t = rho * CumMarketVol_t
POVGap_t = CumExec_t - POVTargetCum_t


2.10 Dark pools and internalisation proxies
For equities:
DPShare_t = OffExchangeVol_t / (TotalVol_t + eps)
ATSShare_t = ATSVol_t / (TotalVol_t + eps)


For fragmented OTC FX/XAUUSD:
VenueShare_{v,t} = Vol_{v,t} / (sum_j Vol_{j,t} + eps)
VenueMixEntropy_t = -sum_v VenueShare_{v,t} * ln(VenueShare_{v,t} + eps)
InternalisationProxy_t = InternalMatchedFlow_t / (TotalObservedFlow_t + eps)


If InternalMatchedFlow_t is unavailable:
InternalisationProxy_t = 1 - ExternalHedgeFlow_t / (TotalClientFlow_t + eps)


QuoteFade_t = CancelledQuotedSize_t / (PostedQuotedSize_t + eps)


Trend raw signal bank
Trend raw signals are computed separately. Only one becomes TrendSel_t in CategorySelectedVector_t.


3.1 VWAP and TWAP trend distance
VWAP_t = VWAP_[sessionStart,t]
TWAP_t = TWAP_[sessionStart,t]
VWAPDist_t = mid_t - VWAP_t
TWAPDist_t = mid_t - TWAP_t


3.2 EMAs
alpha_n = 2 / (n + 1)
EMA_n,t = alpha_n * C_t + (1 - alpha_n) * EMA_n,t-1
EMAFast_t = EMA_{nFast,t}
EMASlow_t = EMA_{nSlow,t}
EMASpread_t = EMAFast_t - EMASlow_t
EMASlope_t = EMA_n,t - EMA_n,t-1


3.3 ADX trend strength
PlusDM_t = max(H_t - H_{t-1}, 0) * I(H_t - H_{t-1} > L_{t-1} - L_t)
MinusDM_t = max(L_{t-1} - L_t, 0) * I(L_{t-1} - L_t > H_t - H_{t-1})
TR_t = max(H_t - L_t, abs(H_t - C_{t-1}), abs(L_t - C_{t-1}))
PlusDI_t = 100 * Sm(PlusDM_t) / (Sm(TR_t) + eps)
MinusDI_t = 100 * Sm(MinusDM_t) / (Sm(TR_t) + eps)
DX_t = 100 * abs(PlusDI_t - MinusDI_t) / (PlusDI_t + MinusDI_t + eps)
ADX_t = Sm(DX_t)


3.4 Trendline regression
price_t = a + b * t + err_t
TrendSlope_t = b


Momentum raw signal bank
Momentum raw signals are computed separately. Only one becomes MomSel_t in CategorySelectedVector_t.


4.1 RSI
RS_t = AvgGain_t / (AvgLoss_t + eps)
RSI_t = 100 - 100 / (1 + RS_t)


4.2 MACD
MACD_t = EMA12(C_t) - EMA26(C_t)
Signal_t = EMA9(MACD_t)
Hist_t = MACD_t - Signal_t


4.3 StochRSI
StochRSI_t = 100 * (RSI_t - min(RSI_{t-n+1..t})) / (max(RSI_{t-n+1..t}) - min(RSI_{t-n+1..t}) + eps)


4.4 ROC
ROC_t = 100 * (C_t / (C_{t-n} + eps) - 1)


Volume raw signal bank
Volume raw signals are computed separately. Only one becomes VolSel_t in CategorySelectedVector_t.


5.1 VSA
BarSpread_t = H_t - L_t
CLV_t = (2 * C_t - H_t - L_t) / (H_t - L_t + eps)
VolZ_t = (V_t - mean(V)) / (stdev(V) + eps)
ER_t = ((C_t - O_t) / (ATR_t + eps)) * VolZ_t


5.2 Footprint
Delta_{p,t} = Vplus_{p,t} - Vminus_{p,t}
FootprintDelta_t = sum_p Delta_{p,t}
ImbAsk_{p,t} = Vplus_{p,t} / (Vminus_{p-dp,t} + eps)
ImbBid_{p,t} = Vminus_{p,t} / (Vplus_{p+dp,t} + eps)
StackedImbalance_t = 1 if there exist k consecutive price levels such that ImbAsk_{p,t} > thetaImb or ImbBid_{p,t} > thetaImb else 0


5.3 Volume Profile and Market Profile
VP_j = sum_{i : p_i in bin_j} vol_i
POC_t = argmax_j VP_j
Construct Value Area by expanding from POC until the cumulative volume reaches the target fraction
POCDist_t = (C_t - POC_t) / (ATR_t + eps)
VAState_t = 1 if C_t > VAH_t, 0 if VAL_t <= C_t <= VAH_t, -1 if C_t < VAL_t


TPO_j = sum_{tau in session} I(price_tau in bin_j)
TPOState_t = 1 if C_t > TPO_VAH_t, 0 if TPO_VAL_t <= C_t <= TPO_VAH_t, -1 if C_t < TPO_VAL_t


Volatility raw signal bank
Volatility raw signals are computed separately. Only one becomes VolaSel_t in CategorySelectedVector_t.


6.1 Realized and range-based volatility
RV_t = sum_{i=1..M} r_{t,i}^2
sigmaP_t^2 = (1 / (4 * ln(2))) * (1 / n) * sum_{j=1..n} (ln(H_j / L_j))^2
sigmaP_t = sqrt(max(sigmaP_t^2, 0))
sigmaGK_t^2 = (1 / n) * sum_{j=1..n} (0.5 * (ln(H_j / L_j))^2 - (2 * ln(2) - 1) * (ln(C_j / O_j))^2)
sigmaGK_t = sqrt(max(sigmaGK_t^2, 0))
mu1 = sqrt(2 / pi)
BV_t = mu1^(-2) * sum_{i=2..M} abs(r_i) * abs(r_{i-1})
Jump_t = max(RV_t - BV_t, 0)


6.2 ATR
ATR_t = Sm(TR_t)


6.3 Bollinger Bands
SMA_n,t = (1 / n) * sum_{i=0..n-1} C_{t-i}
Std_n,t = sqrt((1 / n) * sum_{i=0..n-1} (C_{t-i} - SMA_n,t)^2)
BBUpper_t = SMA_n,t + kBB * Std_n,t
BBLower_t = SMA_n,t - kBB * Std_n,t
BBWidth_t = (BBUpper_t - BBLower_t) / (SMA_n,t + eps)
BBPos_t = (C_t - BBLower_t) / (BBUpper_t - BBLower_t + eps)


Market and Institutional Structures
These are always computed and always appended to FinalIntegratedStateVector_t. They are not subject to one-of-N category selection.


7.1 Liquidity, hunts, stops, grabs, sweeps, spread, slippage, depth fade
BullishSweep_t = 1 if L_t < SwingLowPool_t - epsL and C_t > SwingLowPool_t else 0
BearishSweep_t = 1 if H_t > SwingHighPool_t + epsL and C_t < SwingHighPool_t else 0
SweepScore_t = w1 * (ExcursionBeyondPool_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + w2 * (ReversalDistance_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + w3 * abs(DeltaCVD_t) + w4 * abs(OFI_t)
SpreadShock_t = (spread_t - mean(spread)) / (stdev(spread) + eps)
Slippage_t = sign(side_t) * (Pexec_t - Pdecision_t) / tick
Depth_t = DOMTot_t^(K)
DepthFade_t = 1 - Depth_t / (mean(Depth) + eps)


7.2 Supply and Demand zones
RangeBase_t = HighBase_t - LowBase_t
DemandZoneCandidate_t = 1 if RangeBase_t < betaSD * ATR_t and (Close_{t+h} - HighBase_t) / (ATR_t + eps) > kappaSD else 0
SDScore_t = wFresh * Freshness_t + wImpulse * ImpulseStrength_t + wUnfilled * UnfilledFraction_t - wRetest * RetestCount_t


7.3 Order Blocks
BullishOB_t = [Low_{cstar}, High_{cstar}]
OBScore_t = w1 * Displacement_t + w2 * BOS_t + w3 * ImbalanceAdjacency_t + w4 * VolumeConfirm_t - w5 * Mitigation_t


7.4 Wyckoff and market phases
W_t in {accumulation, markup, distribution, markdown, manipulation}
If probabilistic:
WyckoffScore_t = P_t(markup) + P_t(accumulation) - P_t(markdown) - P_t(distribution)
If rule-based:
WyckoffScore_t = mapped signed phase score in [-1, 1]


7.5 Fair Value Gaps
BullishFVG_t = 1 if Low_t > High_{t-2} else 0
BullishFVGZone_t = [High_{t-2}, Low_t]
GapSize_t = Low_t - High_{t-2}
Mit_t = FilledLength_t / (GapSize_t + eps)
UnfilledFrac_t = 1 - Mit_t
FVGScore_t = wGap * (GapSize_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + wOpen * UnfilledFrac_t + wHTF * HTFAlignment_t - wMit * Mit_t


7.6 Pivot levels
PP_t = (H_prev + L_prev + C_prev) / 3
R1_t = 2 * PP_t - L_prev
S1_t = 2 * PP_t - H_prev
R2_t = PP_t + (H_prev - L_prev)
S2_t = PP_t - (H_prev - L_prev)
PivotDist_t = min(abs(C_t - PP_t), abs(C_t - R1_t), abs(C_t - S1_t), abs(C_t - R2_t), abs(C_t - S2_t)) / (ATR_t + eps)


7.7 Objective Support and Resistance
SR_j = cluster centers of swing extrema, profile nodes, and pivots

After a detailed audit of the chinopardo/CA_Trading_System_EA repository against the Backend Integration Plan specs, I found that many of the core architectural concepts (computing a large raw signal bank, category selection and pass logic, assembling context vectors, and gating by signal‑stack and location) are present, but the repo lacks the comprehensive fallback/degrade logic and proxy substitution required by the new spec. Below is a structured analysis.


1. Existing Implementation Highlights


Raw Signal Inventory – InstitutionalStateVector.mqh builds a large raw signal bank (ISV_SLOT_COUNT enumerations) covering order book depth, order flow, OBI, OFI, execution quality proxies, VWAP/TWAP deviations, ATR, Bollinger bands, RSI/MACD indicators, volume profile, liquidity sweeps, structure scores, Wyckoff phase, FVG scores, momentum and trend indicators, correlation, pivot/Fibonacci distances, and more. These slots correspond to the raw signals specified in the plan. After computing these raw signals, they are normalized via rolling‑window z‑scores and stored in Result.raw[] and Result.z[].

Category Selection – CategorySelector.mqh computes CategorySelectedVector_t for the five categories (Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume, Volatility). It implements fixed vs. dynamic selection modes. In dynamic mode, the Institutional category is split into subfamilies (order‑book, trade flow, impact and execution quality) and the best signal in each subfamily is selected, then the overall winner is chosen by weighted magnitudegithub.com. For other categories, it selects the candidate with the largest absolute z‑score weighted by configuration parametersgithub.com.

Category Pass Logic – After selecting the active signal in each category, ComputeCategoryPasses() maps each selected signal into a pass/fail flag based on directional alignment and magnitude thresholds; for the volatility category it applies a regime map instead of directional signgithub.com. The total number of passing categories (signal_stack_score) is compared against a configured sigsel_min_category_votes to set signal_stack_gategithub.com. Location gating is then computed from pivot distance, support/resistance distance, Fibonacci distance, SD/OB/FVG/sweep/Wyckoff scores etc., producing location_passgithub.com.

Final Integrated State Vector & Heads – AssembleFinalVector() concatenates the base context, selected category values, pass vector, structure vector and auxiliary context vectors into FinalIntegratedStateVector_tgithub.com. BuildHeads() then computes linear weights for alpha, execution and risk, applies the pre‑filter (requiring signal_stack_gate and location_pass), and sets trade_gategithub.com.

Proxy Generation at Feature Level – When order‑book or order‑flow data are missing, InstitutionalStateVector.mqh uses pseudo‑depth proxies (via FillPseudoDepthFromOBI), delta proxies, footprint proxies and profile proxies to populate raw signals. Flags such as dom_proxy_used, flow_proxy_used, footprint_proxy_used and profile_proxy_used indicate whether direct data were unavailable. However, these proxies serve only to fill individual raw features, not to implement category‑level degrade rules.

Policies & Risk Gating – Policies.mqh includes a suite of additional veto checks (session gating, news blocking, spread/volatility/liquidity gating). The file stresses that policies consume upstream canonical gate outcomes and must not rebuild RawSignalBank_t or category vectorsgithub.com.


2. Missing Elements vs. New Spec


The new specification requires significant additional functionality that is absent from the current code. Key missing areas include:


Institutional Availability & Coverage Tracking – The spec mandates variables such as InstAvailable_t, InstCoverage_t, InstPartial_t, InstUnavailable_t, ProxyInstAvailable_t and InstSelSource_t, along with formulas to compute them and degrade when coverage is insufficient. Searches across the repo found no definitions or calculations for these variables, and there is no logic to evaluate coverage across subfamilies or adjust thresholds based on missing signals.

Degrade Modes and Proxy Substitution – The spec outlines four degrade modes (proxy_substitute, proxy_substitute_then_stack_relax, soft_neutral_then_stack_relax, hard_block) and rules for selecting proxy Institutional signals (ProxyInstSel_t) when direct signals are unavailable. There is no code implementing these modes. CategorySelector simply selects the best candidate among available raw Institutional signals; it never substitutes a proxy candidate when the direct Institutional category is unavailable or missing a subfamily.

Dynamic Adjustment of Minimum Category Votes – EffectiveMinCategoryVotes_t should be reduced by one (floored at MinCategoryVotesFloor) when the Institutional category is unavailable or proxies are used, to avoid hard‑blocking trades in FX/XAUUSD. The existing code always uses cfg.sigsel_min_category_votes to compute the stack gategithub.com and never adjusts it based on proxy usage or availability.

Proxy Institutional Candidates & Composite Proxy Score – The spec defines a set of proxy Institutional signals (e.g., microprice bias, auction bias, liquidity stress composite) and requires computing a proxy composite and selecting the best proxy candidate. None of these proxy signals (except microprice and some liquidity measures used as structure features) are treated as candidates; there is no ProxyInstCandidates_t or ProxyInstSel_t.

Relaxation / Hard Block Logic – When the Institutional category is completely unavailable and no proxy exists, the spec calls for a “hard block” (prevent trading) or “soft neutral” degrade. The current implementation never blocks trades due to missing Institutional data. It only sets dom_proxy_used or flow_proxy_used flags and continues to select the best available candidate or proxies at the feature level.

Coverage and Degrade Context Vector – The spec requires constructing a CoverageContextVector_t and optionally AuxContextVector_t with z‑scores and availability flags to inform the decision heads. The existing code does include an auxiliary context vector (venue mix entropy, DP/ATS shares, internalisation proxy, quote fade, correlation)github.com, but there is no coverage context vector capturing Institutional availability and degrade mode.

Explicit Category Membership List & EMA/ATR/Bollinger Category – Although the raw bank includes most of the required indicators, there is no explicit enumeration of category membership or mapping functions to assign each raw signal to Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume or Volatility as described in the spec. For instance, EMAs, ATR and Bollinger width are computed but not explicitly assigned to categories or used in selection beyond their z‑scores being available.

Directional Map & Regime Map Based on Plan – CategorySelector uses a magnitude‑only selection and then maps direction with DirMapSelectedSignal for certain indicators. The spec lists specific directional mappings for RSI, StochRSI, ADX, etc., but these are not explicitly coded; the current pass logic uses generic DirMapSelectedSignal and threshold comparisons.


3. Conclusion


The current CA_Trading_System_EA implementation achieves a robust signal‑stack architecture, including a comprehensive raw signal bank, category selection and pass gating, and integration into alpha, execution and risk heads. However, it does not implement the new fallback/degrade logic, Institutional availability tracking, proxy substitution or dynamic relaxation of minimum category votes mandated by the “Backend Integration Plan.” The code lacks the new variables and decision branches needed to support FX/XAUUSD fragmentation and degrade gracefully when Institutional data are missing. To comply with the plan, extensive modifications are required, including:


Implementing variables for Institutional availability and coverage and adding logic to compute them.

Adding proxy Institutional candidates and composite proxy scores.

Introducing degrade modes with configurable behaviour (proxy_substitute, proxy_substitute_then_stack_relax, soft_neutral_then_stack_relax, hard_block).

Adjusting the signal‑stack gate threshold based on degrade modes.

Building coverage and degrade context vectors and appending them to the final state vector.

Ensuring one‑of‑N selection per category still respects anti‑echo subfamilies but uses proxies or relaxes thresholds when necessary.


Until these enhancements are made, the system will continue to rely on feature‑level proxies and may produce trades even when the entire Institutional category is unavailable, contrary to the requirements of the new spec.

After a detailed audit of the `chinopardo/CA_Trading_System_EA` repository against the **Backend Integration Plan** specs, I found that many of the core architectural concepts (computing a large raw signal bank, category selection and pass logic, assembling context vectors, and gating by signal‑stack and location) are present, but the repo lacks the comprehensive fallback/degrade logic and proxy substitution required by the new spec. Below is a structured analysis. ### 1\. Existing Implementation Highlights * **Raw Signal Inventory** – `InstitutionalStateVector.mqh` builds a large raw signal bank (`ISV_SLOT_COUNT` enumerations) covering order book depth, order flow, OBI, OFI, execution quality proxies, VWAP/TWAP deviations, ATR, Bollinger bands, RSI/MACD indicators, volume profile, liquidity sweeps, structure scores, Wyckoff phase, FVG scores, momentum and trend indicators, correlation, pivot/Fibonacci distances, and more. These slots correspond to the raw signals specified in the plan. After computing these raw signals, they are normalized via rolling‑window z‑scores and stored in `Result.raw[]` and `Result.z[]`. * **Category Selection** – `CategorySelector.mqh` computes `CategorySelectedVector_t` for the five categories (Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume, Volatility). It implements **fixed vs. dynamic** selection modes. In dynamic mode, the Institutional category is split into subfamilies (order‑book, trade flow, impact and execution quality) and the best signal in each subfamily is selected, then the overall winner is chosen by weighted magnitude[github.com](https://github.com/chinopardo/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/blob/HEAD/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/include/CategorySelector.mqh#L260-L354). For other categories, it selects the candidate with the largest absolute z‑score weighted by configuration parameters[github.com](https://github.com/chinopardo/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/blob/HEAD/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/include/CategorySelector.mqh#L356-L388). * **Category Pass Logic** – After selecting the active signal in each category, `ComputeCategoryPasses()` maps each selected signal into a pass/fail flag based on directional alignment and magnitude thresholds; for the volatility category it applies a regime map instead of directional sign[github.com](https://github.com/chinopardo/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/blob/HEAD/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/include/CategorySelector.mqh#L521-L538). The total number of passing categories (`signal_stack_score`) is compared against a configured `sigsel_min_category_votes` to set `signal_stack_gate`[github.com](https://github.com/chinopardo/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/blob/HEAD/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/include/CategorySelector.mqh#L541-L552). Location gating is then computed from pivot distance, support/resistance distance, Fibonacci distance, SD/OB/FVG/sweep/Wyckoff scores etc., producing `location_pass`[github.com](https://github.com/chinopardo/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/blob/HEAD/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/include/CategorySelector.mqh#L554-L584). * **Final Integrated State Vector & Heads** – `AssembleFinalVector()` concatenates the base context, selected category values, pass vector, structure vector and auxiliary context vectors into `FinalIntegratedStateVector_t`[github.com](https://github.com/chinopardo/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/blob/HEAD/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/include/CategorySelector.mqh#L634-L654). `BuildHeads()` then computes linear weights for alpha, execution and risk, applies the pre‑filter (requiring `signal_stack_gate` and `location_pass`), and sets `trade_gate`[github.com](https://github.com/chinopardo/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/blob/HEAD/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/include/InstitutionalStateVector.mqh#L1048-L1186). * **Proxy Generation at Feature Level** – When order‑book or order‑flow data are missing, `InstitutionalStateVector.mqh` uses pseudo‑depth proxies (via `FillPseudoDepthFromOBI`), delta proxies, footprint proxies and profile proxies to populate raw signals. Flags such as `dom_proxy_used`, `flow_proxy_used`, `footprint_proxy_used` and `profile_proxy_used` indicate whether direct data were unavailable. However, these proxies serve only to fill individual raw features, not to implement category‑level degrade rules. * **Policies & Risk Gating** – `Policies.mqh` includes a suite of additional veto checks (session gating, news blocking, spread/volatility/liquidity gating). The file stresses that policies consume upstream canonical gate outcomes and must not rebuild `RawSignalBank_t` or category vectors[github.com](https://github.com/chinopardo/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/blob/5743ac78b53befb6a97899c28ce3f32cbb70c3b0/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/include/Policies.mqh#L805-L819). ### 2\. Missing Elements vs. New Spec The new specification requires significant additional functionality that is **absent** from the current code. Key missing areas include: 1. **Institutional Availability & Coverage Tracking** – The spec mandates variables such as `InstAvailable_t`, `InstCoverage_t`, `InstPartial_t`, `InstUnavailable_t`, `ProxyInstAvailable_t` and `InstSelSource_t`, along with formulas to compute them and degrade when coverage is insufficient. Searches across the repo found no definitions or calculations for these variables, and there is no logic to evaluate coverage across subfamilies or adjust thresholds based on missing signals. 2. **Degrade Modes and Proxy Substitution** – The spec outlines four degrade modes (`proxy_substitute`, `proxy_substitute_then_stack_relax`, `soft_neutral_then_stack_relax`, `hard_block`) and rules for selecting proxy Institutional signals (`ProxyInstSel_t`) when direct signals are unavailable. There is no code implementing these modes. `CategorySelector` simply selects the best candidate among available raw Institutional signals; it never substitutes a proxy candidate when the direct Institutional category is unavailable or missing a subfamily. 3. **Dynamic Adjustment of Minimum Category Votes** – `EffectiveMinCategoryVotes_t` should be reduced by one (floored at `MinCategoryVotesFloor`) when the Institutional category is unavailable or proxies are used, to avoid hard‑blocking trades in FX/XAUUSD. The existing code always uses `cfg.sigsel_min_category_votes` to compute the stack gate[github.com](https://github.com/chinopardo/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/blob/HEAD/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/include/CategorySelector.mqh#L548-L552) and never adjusts it based on proxy usage or availability. 4. **Proxy Institutional Candidates & Composite Proxy Score** – The spec defines a set of proxy Institutional signals (e.g., microprice bias, auction bias, liquidity stress composite) and requires computing a proxy composite and selecting the best proxy candidate. None of these proxy signals (except microprice and some liquidity measures used as structure features) are treated as candidates; there is no `ProxyInstCandidates_t` or `ProxyInstSel_t`. 5. **Relaxation / Hard Block Logic** – When the Institutional category is completely unavailable and no proxy exists, the spec calls for a “hard block” (prevent trading) or “soft neutral” degrade. The current implementation never blocks trades due to missing Institutional data. It only sets `dom_proxy_used` or `flow_proxy_used` flags and continues to select the best available candidate or proxies at the feature level. 6. **Coverage and Degrade Context Vector** – The spec requires constructing a `CoverageContextVector_t` and optionally `AuxContextVector_t` with z‑scores and availability flags to inform the decision heads. The existing code does include an auxiliary context vector (venue mix entropy, DP/ATS shares, internalisation proxy, quote fade, correlation)[github.com](https://github.com/chinopardo/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/blob/HEAD/CA\_Trading\_System\_EA/include/CategorySelector.mqh#L624-L632), but there is no coverage context vector capturing Institutional availability and degrade mode. 7. **Explicit Category Membership List & EMA/ATR/Bollinger Category** – Although the raw bank includes most of the required indicators, there is no explicit enumeration of category membership or mapping functions to assign each raw signal to Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume or Volatility as described in the spec. For instance, EMAs, ATR and Bollinger width are computed but not explicitly assigned to categories or used in selection beyond their z‑scores being available. 8. **Directional Map & Regime Map Based on Plan** – `CategorySelector` uses a magnitude‑only selection and then maps direction with `DirMapSelectedSignal` for certain indicators. The spec lists specific directional mappings for RSI, StochRSI, ADX, etc., but these are not explicitly coded; the current pass logic uses generic `DirMapSelectedSignal` and threshold comparisons. ### 3\. Conclusion The current `CA_Trading_System_EA` implementation achieves a robust **signal‑stack architecture**, including a comprehensive raw signal bank, category selection and pass gating, and integration into alpha, execution and risk heads. However, it **does not implement** the new fallback/degrade logic, Institutional availability tracking, proxy substitution or dynamic relaxation of minimum category votes mandated by the “Backend Integration Plan.” The code lacks the new variables and decision branches needed to support FX/XAUUSD fragmentation and degrade gracefully when Institutional data are missing. To comply with the plan, extensive modifications are required, including: * Implementing variables for Institutional availability and coverage and adding logic to compute them. * Adding proxy Institutional candidates and composite proxy scores. * Introducing degrade modes with configurable behaviour (`proxy_substitute`, `proxy_substitute_then_stack_relax`, `soft_neutral_then_stack_relax`, `hard_block`). * Adjusting the signal‑stack gate threshold based on degrade modes. * Building coverage and degrade context vectors and appending them to the final state vector. * Ensuring one‑of‑N selection per category still respects anti‑echo subfamilies but uses proxies or relaxes thresholds when necessary. Until these enhancements are made, the system will continue to rely on feature‑level proxies and may produce trades even when the entire Institutional category is unavailable, contrary to the requirements of the new spec.

Goal:

Context:
For FOREX and XAUUSD, assume OTC venue fragmentation. Spot FX and most FX derivatives are OTC, decentralized, fragmented across venues, and much customer flow is internalised rather than fully visible to the broader market. BIS describes FX as OTC, decentralized, fragmented, and increasingly internalised, while LBMA describes the London gold market as a wholesale OTC market and publishes trade data to improve transparency rather than offering a single consolidated public order book. U.S. ATS and off-exchange transparency is delayed, not real-time. Use Cont-Kukanov-Stoikov style OFI and depth-impact structure. Use standard TA-Lib-style RSI, MACD, ADX, ATR, StochRSI, EMA, and Bollinger definitions. The architecture must avoid indicator echo by selecting one active signal from each category, then combining those selected category signals with always-on Market/Institutional Structures for FinalIntegratedStateVector_t. The selected category signals must feed a mandatory SignalStackGate_t and a mandatory LocationPass_t before alpha, execution, and risk routing. The fallback/degrade logic must never fabricate missing DOM, Order Flow, OBI, OFI, or trade-sign truth from candle-only data; it must first use valid direct signals, then valid proxy signals, then explicit stack-relax or hard-block rules. 6.1 Realized and range-based volatility
RV_t = sum_{i=1..M} r_{t,i}^2
sigmaP_t^2 = (1 / (4 * ln(2))) * (1 / n) * sum_{j=1..n} (ln(H_j / L_j))^2
sigmaP_t = sqrt(max(sigmaP_t^2, 0))
sigmaGK_t^2 = (1 / n) * sum_{j=1..n} (0.5 * (ln(H_j / L_j))^2 - (2 * ln(2) - 1) * (ln(C_j / O_j))^2)
sigmaGK_t = sqrt(max(sigmaGK_t^2, 0))
mu1 = sqrt(2 / pi)
BV_t = mu1^(-2) * sum_{i=2..M} abs(r_i) * abs(r_{i-1})
Jump_t = max(RV_t - BV_t, 0)


6.2 ATR
ATR_t = Sm(TR_t)


6.3 Bollinger Bands
SMA_n,t = (1 / n) * sum_{i=0..n-1} C_{t-i}
Std_n,t = sqrt((1 / n) * sum_{i=0..n-1} (C_{t-i} - SMA_n,t)^2)
BBUpper_t = SMA_n,t + kBB * Std_n,t
BBLower_t = SMA_n,t - kBB * Std_n,t
BBWidth_t = (BBUpper_t - BBLower_t) / (SMA_n,t + eps)
BBPos_t = (C_t - BBLower_t) / (BBUpper_t - BBLower_t + eps)


Market and Institutional Structures
These are always computed and always appended to FinalIntegratedStateVector_t. They are not subject to one-of-N category selection.


7.1 Liquidity, hunts, stops, grabs, sweeps, spread, slippage, depth fade
BullishSweep_t = 1 if L_t < SwingLowPool_t - epsL and C_t > SwingLowPool_t else 0
BearishSweep_t = 1 if H_t > SwingHighPool_t + epsL and C_t < SwingHighPool_t else 0
SweepScore_t = w1 * (ExcursionBeyondPool_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + w2 * (ReversalDistance_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + w3 * abs(DeltaCVD_t) + w4 * abs(OFI_t)
SpreadShock_t = (spread_t - mean(spread)) / (stdev(spread) + eps)
Slippage_t = sign(side_t) * (Pexec_t - Pdecision_t) / tick
Depth_t = DOMTot_t^(K)
DepthFade_t = 1 - Depth_t / (mean(Depth) + eps)


7.2 Supply and Demand zones
RangeBase_t = HighBase_t - LowBase_t
DemandZoneCandidate_t = 1 if RangeBase_t < betaSD * ATR_t and (Close_{t+h} - HighBase_t) / (ATR_t + eps) > kappaSD else 0
SDScore_t = wFresh * Freshness_t + wImpulse * ImpulseStrength_t + wUnfilled * UnfilledFraction_t - wRetest * RetestCount_t


7.3 Order Blocks
BullishOB_t = [Low_{cstar}, High_{cstar}]
OBScore_t = w1 * Displacement_t + w2 * BOS_t + w3 * ImbalanceAdjacency_t + w4 * VolumeConfirm_t - w5 * Mitigation_t


7.4 Wyckoff and market phases
W_t in {accumulation, markup, distribution, markdown, manipulation}
If probabilistic:
WyckoffScore_t = P_t(markup) + P_t(accumulation) - P_t(markdown) - P_t(distribution)
If rule-based:
WyckoffScore_t = mapped signed phase score in [-1, 1]


7.5 Fair Value Gaps
BullishFVG_t = 1 if Low_t > High_{t-2} else 0
BullishFVGZone_t = [High_{t-2}, Low_t]
GapSize_t = Low_t - High_{t-2}
Mit_t = FilledLength_t / (GapSize_t + eps)
UnfilledFrac_t = 1 - Mit_t
FVGScore_t = wGap * (GapSize_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + wOpen * UnfilledFrac_t + wHTF * HTFAlignment_t - wMit * Mit_t


7.6 Pivot levels
PP_t = (H_prev + L_prev + C_prev) / 3
R1_t = 2 * PP_t - L_prev
S1_t = 2 * PP_t - H_prev
R2_t = PP_t + (H_prev - L_prev)
S2_t = PP_t - (H_prev - L_prev)
PivotDist_t = min(abs(C_t - PP_t), abs(C_t - R1_t), abs(C_t - S1_t), abs(C_t - R2_t), abs(C_t - S2_t)) / (ATR_t + eps)


7.7 Objective Support and Resistance
SR_j = cluster centers of swing extrema, profile nodes, and pivots
SRDist_t = min_j abs(C_t - SR_j) / (ATR_t + eps)


7.8 Fibonacci
Fib_r = H - r * (H - L), where r in {0.236, 0.382, 0.5, 0.618, 0.786}
FibDist_t = min_{r in R} abs(C_t - Fib_r) / (ATR_t + eps)


7.9 Trendlines
price_t = a + b * t + err_t
TrendSlope_t = b


7.10 Correlation
rho_xy,t = sum_{i=1..n} (x_i - xbar) * (y_i - ybar) / (sqrt(sum_{i=1..n} (x_i - xbar)^2) * sqrt(sum_{i=1..n} (y_i - ybar)^2) + eps)

valid_{j,t} = 1 if x_{j,t} is finite and source freshness <= max_age_j and source quality >= qmin_j else 0

Category pass logic and signal stack
Convert selected category signals into pass/fail confirmation states before alpha, execution, and risk routing.


If InstSelSource_t = 2:

VolPass_t = 1 if DirMap(VolSel_t) = Dir_t and abs(z(VolSel_t)) >= thVol else 0

PreFilter_t = 1 if SignalStackGate_t = 1 and LocationPass_t = 1 else 0


alphaRaw_t = wAlpha^T * FinalIntegratedStateVector_t

RiskPass_t = 1 if risk_t < thetaRisk else 0

size_t = sizeMax * f(alphaRaw_t) * g(risk_t) * h(execRaw_t)


Implementation rules
Compute all raw formulas first, then normalize, then run category selection, then run availability and degrade logic, then run category pass logic, then run SignalStackGate_t and LocationPass_t, then assemble the final vector, then evaluate alpha, execution, risk, and TradeGate_t.
Do not merge DOM, Order Flow, OBI, and OFI into one raw variable.
Do not discard non-selected category signals; store them in RawSignalBank_t.
Allow config to switch category selection between fixed and dynamic modes.
Allow config to switch Institutional selection between anti-echo subfamily mode and direct full-category mode.
Allow config to switch Institutional degrade mode between "proxy_substitute", "proxy_substitute_then_stack_relax", "soft_neutral_then_stack_relax", and "hard_block".
Allow config to map any candidate to any category only if explicitly configured; otherwise use the default category membership above.
For FX/XAUUSD, use venue-aware aggregation for DOM, OBI, order flow, and internalisation proxies.
For futures, equities, and crypto, use direct centralized-book logic when available.
Keep Market/Institutional Structures always-on in FinalIntegratedStateVector_t even though the selector only chooses one active signal from each of Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume, and Volatility.
When one Institutional signal is missing, skip only that invalid signal and rescore remaining direct Institutional candidates.
When one Institutional subfamily is missing, keep Institutional selection active from remaining direct subfamilies.
When the whole Institutional category is unavailable:

Use direct proxy substitution if valid proxy Institutional candidates exist and degrade mode allows it.

Else use stack-relax if degrade mode allows it by reducing EffectiveMinCategoryVotes_t by one, floored by MinCategoryVotesFloor.

Else hard block the trade if degrade mode = "hard_block".
Never fabricate DOM, OBI, OFI, signed-flow, or CVD values from candle data when those observables are unavailable.
Preserve every formula above exactly, including RawSignalBank_t, selector logic, availability logic, degrade logic, pass logic, hard location gate, and final vector assembly.


Implement the full RawSignalBank_t, category selector, Institutional fallback/degrade logic, category pass stack, hard location gate, and FinalIntegratedStateVector_t exactly as specified above.

Below is a comprehensive, per‑file implementation plan for updating the CA_Trading_System_EA repository to fully implement the new Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume and Volatility signal‑stack specification described in the “Backend Integration Plan.” The plan references specific files in the repo (using the current commit 5743ac78) and explains what must be added, modified or created to satisfy the new requirements. Where helpful, existing code lines are cited to show why a change is needed.


1. CA_Trading_System_EA/include/Types.mqh


Expand raw‑signal enumerations. The RawSignalIndex enum currently covers base variables and many microstructure and TA indicators. We must add entries for any new raw signals appearing in the spec (e.g., MicropriceBias, AuctionBias, LiquidityStressProxy, ProxyInstComposite, InstCoverage, etc.). These enumerations drive the size of RawSignalBank_t and must be kept contiguous.

Add degrade and coverage enums/structs. The CategoryPassVector struct currently stores five pass flags plus stack and location info (nine fields). The spec introduces new state variables (InstCoverage_t, InstAvailable_t, InstPartial_t, InstUnavailable_t, ProxyInstAvailable_t, InstSelSource_t, HardInstBlock_t, EffectiveMinCategoryVotes_t). Extend CategoryPassVector to include these fields and update SIGSEL_CATEGORY_PASS_SIZE to reflect the larger size.

Define a CoverageContextVector struct containing the z‑score of InstCoverage_t plus the remaining degrade flags, so this vector can be appended unconditionally to FinalIntegratedStateVector_t.

Extend FinalIntegratedStateVector_t. At present the final state vector concatenates base context, selected categories, pass vector, structure vector and auxiliary contextgithub.com. Add the new CoverageContextVector and ensure a “compact mode” still works by conditionally excluding the auxiliary vector only. Update FINAL_VECTOR_SIZE constants accordingly.

Define degrade‑mode enum. Create an enumeration for InstitutionalDegradeMode with values "proxy_substitute", "proxy_substitute_then_stack_relax", "soft_neutral_then_stack_relax" and "hard_block". This enum should be available throughout the code.

Summary


Implementing the new specification requires broad changes across the repo. The most significant work lies in CategorySelector.mqh, where coverage, proxy substitution and degrade modes must be implemented, and in InstitutionalStateVector.mqh, which must compute new signals and assemble the expanded final state vector. Supporting changes in Types.mqh, Config.mqh, the main EA file, and policy/strategy modules ensure that the new signals and degrade logic propagate correctly through to execution. These modifications will allow the EA to compute a full raw signal bank, select exactly one signal per category, apply a rigorous pass/fail stack and location gate, and degrade gracefully (or hard‑block) in OTC markets when Institutional data are incomplete, fulfilling the requirements of the new plan.
